revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0955|death_date={{death date|1019|6|4}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11450303]] Wikidata name: Yoshimasa Abe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Abe no Yoshimasa''' (安倍 吉昌) was a mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat, [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]], and practitioner of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]]. He was the second son of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] (according to some accounts) and held positions such as {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (Doctor of Astronomy), {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Shukeitō|ja|主計頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukeitō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}, and {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|qq=|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. He was granted the Rank of ''Jushiijo'' (従四位上). Alongside his elder brother, {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}}, and other prominent Onmyōji such as {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, he played a central role in leading the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the field of Onmyōdō.
== Career == Yoshimasa began his career in [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1 (970), when [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] recommended him as a ''Tenmon Tokugōsei'' (student specializing in astronomy).<ref>''{{ill|Ruiju Fusenshō|ja|類聚符宣抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruiju Fusenshō|qq=|1=Ruijufusenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665188}}''</ref> In [[:en:Kanna (era)|Kanwa]] 2 (986), he succeeded his father as Tenmon Hakase. By [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] 1 (1004), he was appointed Onmyō no Kami, the head of the Onmyōryō.
Historically, the position of Onmyō no Kami had been reserved for prestigious families such as the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}. However, following Yoshimasa’s appointment, the role began alternating between the Abe and Kamo clans, eventually becoming the hereditary domain of the Abe clan (later known as the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]).
Although Yoshimasa lacks the legendary anecdotes associated with his father and elder brother, his appointment as Onmyō no Kami demonstrates his significant skill and accomplishments. Notably, entries in ''{{ill|Shōyūki|ja|小右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyūki|qq=|1=Shōyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459316}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Sanesuke|Fujiwara no Sanesuke]] indicate that Yoshimasa continued serving as Tenmon Hakase until his death on [[:en:Kannin|Kan'nin]] 3, April 28 (June 4, 1019).<ref>''Shōyūki''</ref> After his passing, a replacement candidate from Iyo Province, named "Kyuho" (秦久邦), was considered but deemed unqualified due to insufficient knowledge, highlighting a talent shortage and the growing influence of local Onmyōji outside the capital.
== Debate on Heirship == While tradition generally identifies {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}} as Abe no Seimei's eldest son and heir, some scholars propose Yoshimasa as the true heir. This view stems from the nearly identical birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa, as well as Yoshimasa’s appointment as Onmyō no Kami—a role typically reserved for the eldest son in a hereditary system.<ref>Examples of this debate are reflected in modern interpretations, such as the manga ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' by [[:en:Reiko Okano|Reiko Okano]], where Yoshimasa is depicted as Seimei's eldest son.</ref>
However, historical records such as ''Shōyūki'' and ''{{ill|Mido Kanpakuki|ja|御堂関白記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mido Kanpakuki|qq=|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488903}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] reference Yoshihira more frequently than Yoshimasa, suggesting that Yoshihira was widely recognized as Seimei’s successor. Additionally, Yoshimasa’s son, {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is sometimes identified as Yoshihira’s biological child adopted by Yoshimasa.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu'' (''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}'').</ref>
== Family == * Father: [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}

[[ja:安倍吉昌]]
<!--Yoshimasa--><!--Yoshimasa--><!--Yoshimasa--> [[Category:Abe Clan]] [[Category:Aristocrats of the Mid-Heian Period]] [[Category:Recipients of Lower Junior Fourth Rank]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11450303}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0955|death_date={{death date|1019|6|4}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11450303]] Wikidata name: Yoshimasa Abe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Abe no Yoshimasa''' (安倍 吉昌) was a mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat, [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]], and practitioner of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]]. He was the second son of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] (according to some accounts) and held positions such as {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (Doctor of Astronomy), {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Shukeitō|ja|主計頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukeitō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}, and {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|qq=|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. He was granted the Rank of ''Jushiijo'' (従四位上). Alongside his elder brother, {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}}, and other prominent Onmyōji such as {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, he played a central role in leading the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the field of Onmyōdō.
== Career == Yoshimasa began his career in [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1 (970), when [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] recommended him as a ''Tenmon Tokugōsei'' (student specializing in astronomy).<ref>''{{ill|Ruiju Fusenshō|ja|類聚符宣抄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruiju Fusenshō|qq=|1=Ruijufusenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665188}}''</ref> In [[:en:Kanna (era)|Kanwa]] 2 (986), he succeeded his father as Tenmon Hakase. By [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] 1 (1004), he was appointed Onmyō no Kami, the head of the Onmyōryō.
Historically, the position of Onmyō no Kami had been reserved for prestigious families such as the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}. However, following Yoshimasa’s appointment, the role began alternating between the Abe and Kamo clans, eventually becoming the hereditary domain of the Abe clan (later known as the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]).
Although Yoshimasa lacks the legendary anecdotes associated with his father and elder brother, his appointment as Onmyō no Kami demonstrates his significant skill and accomplishments. Notably, entries in ''{{ill|Shōyūki|ja|小右記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyūki|qq=|1=Shōyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459316}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Sanesuke|Fujiwara no Sanesuke]] indicate that Yoshimasa continued serving as Tenmon Hakase until his death on [[:en:Kannin|Kan'nin]] 3, April 28 (June 4, 1019).<ref>''Shōyūki''</ref> After his passing, a replacement candidate from Iyo Province, named "Kyuho" (秦久邦), was considered but deemed unqualified due to insufficient knowledge, highlighting a talent shortage and the growing influence of local Onmyōji outside the capital.
== Debate on Heirship == While tradition generally identifies {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}} as Abe no Seimei's eldest son and heir, some scholars propose Yoshimasa as the true heir. This view stems from the nearly identical birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa, as well as Yoshimasa’s appointment as Onmyō no Kami—a role typically reserved for the eldest son in a hereditary system.<ref>Examples of this debate are reflected in modern interpretations, such as the manga ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' by [[:en:Reiko Okano|Reiko Okano]], where Yoshimasa is depicted as Seimei's eldest son.</ref>
However, historical records such as ''Shōyūki'' and ''{{ill|Mido Kanpakuki|ja|御堂関白記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mido Kanpakuki|qq=|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488903}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] reference Yoshihira more frequently than Yoshimasa, suggesting that Yoshihira was widely recognized as Seimei’s successor. Additionally, Yoshimasa’s son, {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is sometimes identified as Yoshihira’s biological child adopted by Yoshimasa.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu'' (''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}'').</ref>
== Family == * Father: [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>

He was the second son of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] (according to some accounts) and held positions such as {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (Doctor of Astronomy), {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Shukeitō|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukeitō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}, and {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. He was granted the Rank of ''Jushiijo'' (従四位上). Alongside his elder brother, {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}}, and other prominent Onmyōji such as {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, he played a central role in leading the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the field of Onmyōdō.
== Career == Yoshimasa began his career in [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1 (970), when [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] recommended him as a ''Tenmon Tokugōsei'' (student specializing in astronomy).<ref>''{{ill|Ruiju Fusenshō|ja|類聚符宣抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruiju Fusenshō|1=Ruijufusenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665188}}''</ref> In [[:en:Kanna (era)|Kanwa]] 2 (986), he succeeded his father as Tenmon Hakase. By [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] 1 (1004), he was appointed Onmyō no Kami, the head of the Onmyōryō.
Historically, the position of Onmyō no Kami had been reserved for prestigious families such as the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}. However, following Yoshimasa’s appointment, the role began alternating between the Abe and Kamo clans, eventually becoming the hereditary domain of the Abe clan (later known as the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]).
Although Yoshimasa lacks the legendary anecdotes associated with his father and elder brother, his appointment as Onmyō no Kami demonstrates his significant skill and accomplishments. Notably, entries in ''{{ill|Shōyūki|ja|小右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyūki|1=Shōyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459316}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Sanesuke|Fujiwara no Sanesuke]] indicate that Yoshimasa continued serving as Tenmon Hakase until his death on [[:en:Kannin|Kan'nin]] 3, April 28 (June 4, 1019).<ref>''Shōyūki''</ref> After his passing, a replacement candidate from Iyo Province, named "Kyuho" (秦久邦), was considered but deemed unqualified due to insufficient knowledge, highlighting a talent shortage and the growing influence of local Onmyōji outside the capital.
== Debate on Heirship == While tradition generally identifies {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}} as Abe no Seimei's eldest son and heir, some scholars propose Yoshimasa as the true heir. This view stems from the nearly identical birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa, as well as Yoshimasa’s appointment as Onmyō no Kami—a role typically reserved for the eldest son in a hereditary system.<ref>Examples of this debate are reflected in modern interpretations, such as the manga ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' by [[:en:Reiko Okano|Reiko Okano]], where Yoshimasa is depicted as Seimei's eldest son.</ref>
However, historical records such as ''Shōyūki'' and ''{{ill|Mido Kanpakuki|ja|御堂関白記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mido Kanpakuki|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488903}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] reference Yoshihira more frequently than Yoshimasa, suggesting that Yoshihira was widely recognized as Seimei’s successor. Additionally, Yoshimasa’s son, {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is sometimes identified as Yoshihira’s biological child adopted by Yoshimasa.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu'' (''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}'').</ref>
== Family == * Father: [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>

Notably, entries in ''{{ill|Shōyūki|ja|小右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyūki|1=Shōyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459316}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Sanesuke|Fujiwara no Sanesuke]] indicate that Yoshimasa continued serving as Tenmon Hakase until his death on [[:en:Kannin|Kan'nin]] 3, April 28 (June 4, 1019).<ref>''Shōyūki''</ref> After his passing, a replacement candidate from Iyo Province, named "Kyuho" (秦久邦), was considered but deemed unqualified due to insufficient knowledge, highlighting a talent shortage and the growing influence of local Onmyōji outside the capital.

== Career == Yoshimasa began his career in [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1 (970), when [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] recommended him as a ''Tenmon Tokugōsei'' (student specializing in astronomy).<ref>''{{ill|Ruiju Fusenshō|ja|類聚符宣抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruiju Fusenshō|1=Ruijufusenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665188}}''</ref> In [[:en:Kanna (era)|Kanwa]] 2 (986), he succeeded his father as Tenmon Hakase. By [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] 1 (1004), he was appointed Onmyō no Kami, the head of the Onmyōryō.
Historically, the position of Onmyō no Kami had been reserved for prestigious families such as the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}. However, following Yoshimasa’s appointment, the role began alternating between the Abe and Kamo clans, eventually becoming the hereditary domain of the Abe clan (later known as the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]).
Although Yoshimasa lacks the legendary anecdotes associated with his father and elder brother, his appointment as Onmyō no Kami demonstrates his significant skill and accomplishments. Notably, entries in ''{{ill|Shōyūki|ja|小右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyūki|1=Shōyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459316}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Sanesuke|Fujiwara no Sanesuke]] indicate that Yoshimasa continued serving as Tenmon Hakase until his death on [[:en:Kannin|Kan'nin]] 3, April 28 (June 4, 1019).<ref>''Shōyūki''</ref> After his passing, a replacement candidate from Iyo Province, named "Kyuho" (秦久邦), was considered but deemed unqualified due to insufficient knowledge, highlighting a talent shortage and the growing influence of local Onmyōji outside the capital.
== Debate on Heirship == While tradition generally identifies {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}} as Abe no Seimei's eldest son and heir, some scholars propose Yoshimasa as the true heir. This view stems from the nearly identical birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa, as well as Yoshimasa’s appointment as Onmyō no Kami—a role typically reserved for the eldest son in a hereditary system.<ref>Examples of this debate are reflected in modern interpretations, such as the manga ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' by [[:en:Reiko Okano|Reiko Okano]], where Yoshimasa is depicted as Seimei's eldest son.</ref>
However, historical records such as ''Shōyūki'' and ''{{ill|Mido Kanpakuki|ja|御堂関白記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mido Kanpakuki|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488903}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] reference Yoshihira more frequently than Yoshimasa, suggesting that Yoshihira was widely recognized as Seimei’s successor. Additionally, Yoshimasa’s son, {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is sometimes identified as Yoshihira’s biological child adopted by Yoshimasa.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu'' (''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}'').</ref>
== Family == * Father: [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>

Historically, the position of Onmyō no Kami had been reserved for prestigious families such as the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}. == Debate on Heirship == While tradition generally identifies {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}} as Abe no Seimei's eldest son and heir, some scholars propose Yoshimasa as the true heir. This view stems from the nearly identical birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa, as well as Yoshimasa’s appointment as Onmyō no Kami—a role typically reserved for the eldest son in a hereditary system.<ref>Examples of this debate are reflected in modern interpretations, such as the manga ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' by [[:en:Reiko Okano|Reiko Okano]], where Yoshimasa is depicted as Seimei's eldest son.</ref>
However, historical records such as ''Shōyūki'' and ''{{ill|Mido Kanpakuki|ja|御堂関白記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mido Kanpakuki|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488903}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] reference Yoshihira more frequently than Yoshimasa, suggesting that Yoshihira was widely recognized as Seimei’s successor. Additionally, Yoshimasa’s son, {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is sometimes identified as Yoshihira’s biological child adopted by Yoshimasa.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu'' (''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}'').</ref>
== Family == * Father: [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0955|death_date={{death date|1019|6|4}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11450303]] Wikidata name: Yoshimasa Abe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Abe no Yoshimasa''' (安倍 吉昌) was a mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat, [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]], and practitioner of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]]. He was the second son of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] (according to some accounts) and held positions such as {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (Doctor of Astronomy), {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Shukeitō|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukeitō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}, and {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. He was granted the Rank of ''Jushiijo'' (従四位上). Alongside his elder brother, {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}}, and other prominent Onmyōji such as {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, he played a central role in leading the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the field of Onmyōdō.
== Career == Yoshimasa began his career in [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1 (970), when [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] recommended him as a ''Tenmon Tokugōsei'' (student specializing in astronomy).<ref>''{{ill|Ruiju Fusenshō|ja|類聚符宣抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruiju Fusenshō|1=Ruijufusenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665188}}''</ref> In [[:en:Kanna (era)|Kanwa]] 2 (986), he succeeded his father as Tenmon Hakase. By [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] 1 (1004), he was appointed Onmyō no Kami, the head of the Onmyōryō.
Historically, the position of Onmyō no Kami had been reserved for prestigious families such as the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}. However, following Yoshimasa’s appointment, the role began alternating between the Abe and Kamo clans, eventually becoming the hereditary domain of the Abe clan (later known as the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]).
Although Yoshimasa lacks the legendary anecdotes associated with his father and elder brother, his appointment as Onmyō no Kami demonstrates his significant skill and accomplishments. Notably, entries in ''{{ill|Shōyūki|ja|小右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyūki|1=Shōyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459316}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Sanesuke|Fujiwara no Sanesuke]] indicate that Yoshimasa continued serving as Tenmon Hakase until his death on [[:en:Kannin|Kan'nin]] 3, April 28 (June 4, 1019).<ref>''Shōyūki''</ref> After his passing, a replacement candidate from Iyo Province, named "Kyuho" (秦久邦), was considered but deemed unqualified due to insufficient knowledge, highlighting a talent shortage and the growing influence of local Onmyōji outside the capital.
== Debate on Heirship == While tradition generally identifies {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}} as Abe no Seimei's eldest son and heir, some scholars propose Yoshimasa as the true heir. This view stems from the nearly identical birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa, as well as Yoshimasa’s appointment as Onmyō no Kami—a role typically reserved for the eldest son in a hereditary system.<ref>Examples of this debate are reflected in modern interpretations, such as the manga ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' by [[:en:Reiko Okano|Reiko Okano]], where Yoshimasa is depicted as Seimei's eldest son.</ref>
However, historical records such as ''Shōyūki'' and ''{{ill|Mido Kanpakuki|ja|御堂関白記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mido Kanpakuki|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488903}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] reference Yoshihira more frequently than Yoshimasa, suggesting that Yoshihira was widely recognized as Seimei’s successor. Additionally, Yoshimasa’s son, {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is sometimes identified as Yoshihira’s biological child adopted by Yoshimasa.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu'' (''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}'').</ref>
== Family == * Father: [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>

== Family == * Father: [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>

<!--Yoshimasa--><!--Yoshimasa--><!--Yoshimasa--> [[Category:Abe Clan]] [[Category:Aristocrats of the Mid-Heian Period]] [[Category:Recipients of Lower Junior Fourth Rank]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11450303}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0955|death_date={{death date|1019|6|4}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11450303]] Wikidata name: Yoshimasa Abe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Abe no Yoshimasa''' (安倍 吉昌) was a mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat, [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]], and practitioner of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]]. He was the second son of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] (according to some accounts) and held positions such as {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (Doctor of Astronomy), {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Shukeitō|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukeitō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}, and {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. He was granted the Rank of ''Jushiijo'' (従四位上). Alongside his elder brother, {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}}, and other prominent Onmyōji such as {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, he played a central role in leading the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the field of Onmyōdō.
== Career == Yoshimasa began his career in [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1 (970), when [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] recommended him as a ''Tenmon Tokugōsei'' (student specializing in astronomy).<ref>''{{ill|Ruiju Fusenshō|ja|類聚符宣抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruiju Fusenshō|1=Ruijufusenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665188}}''</ref> In [[:en:Kanna (era)|Kanwa]] 2 (986), he succeeded his father as Tenmon Hakase. By [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] 1 (1004), he was appointed Onmyō no Kami, the head of the Onmyōryō.
Historically, the position of Onmyō no Kami had been reserved for prestigious families such as the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}. However, following Yoshimasa’s appointment, the role began alternating between the Abe and Kamo clans, eventually becoming the hereditary domain of the Abe clan (later known as the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]).
Although Yoshimasa lacks the legendary anecdotes associated with his father and elder brother, his appointment as Onmyō no Kami demonstrates his significant skill and accomplishments. Notably, entries in ''{{ill|Shōyūki|ja|小右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyūki|1=Shōyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459316}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Sanesuke|Fujiwara no Sanesuke]] indicate that Yoshimasa continued serving as Tenmon Hakase until his death on [[:en:Kannin|Kan'nin]] 3, April 28 (June 4, 1019).<ref>''Shōyūki''</ref> After his passing, a replacement candidate from Iyo Province, named "Kyuho" (秦久邦), was considered but deemed unqualified due to insufficient knowledge, highlighting a talent shortage and the growing influence of local Onmyōji outside the capital.
== Debate on Heirship == While tradition generally identifies {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}} as Abe no Seimei's eldest son and heir, some scholars propose Yoshimasa as the true heir. This view stems from the nearly identical birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa, as well as Yoshimasa’s appointment as Onmyō no Kami—a role typically reserved for the eldest son in a hereditary system.<ref>Examples of this debate are reflected in modern interpretations, such as the manga ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' by [[:en:Reiko Okano|Reiko Okano]], where Yoshimasa is depicted as Seimei's eldest son.</ref>
However, historical records such as ''Shōyūki'' and ''{{ill|Mido Kanpakuki|ja|御堂関白記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mido Kanpakuki|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488903}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] reference Yoshihira more frequently than Yoshimasa, suggesting that Yoshihira was widely recognized as Seimei’s successor. Additionally, Yoshimasa’s son, {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is sometimes identified as Yoshihira’s biological child adopted by Yoshimasa.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu'' (''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}'').</ref>
== Family == * Father: [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0955|death_date={{death date|1019|6|4}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11450303]] Wikidata name: Yoshimasa Abe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Abe no Yoshimasa''' (安倍 吉昌) was a mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] aristocrat, [[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji]], and practitioner of [[:en:Onmyōdō|Onmyōdō]]. He was the second son of [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] (according to some accounts) and held positions such as {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (Doctor of Astronomy), {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Shukeitō|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukeitō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}, and {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. He was granted the Rank of ''Jushiijo'' (従四位上). Alongside his elder brother, {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}}, and other prominent Onmyōji such as {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, he played a central role in leading the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the field of Onmyōdō.
== Career == Yoshimasa began his career in [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1 (970), when [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] recommended him as a ''Tenmon Tokugōsei'' (student specializing in astronomy).<ref>''{{ill|Ruiju Fusenshō|ja|類聚符宣抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruiju Fusenshō|1=Ruijufusenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665188}}''</ref> In [[:en:Kanna (era)|Kanwa]] 2 (986), he succeeded his father as Tenmon Hakase. By [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] 1 (1004), he was appointed Onmyō no Kami, the head of the Onmyōryō.
Historically, the position of Onmyō no Kami had been reserved for prestigious families such as the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}. However, following Yoshimasa’s appointment, the role began alternating between the Abe and Kamo clans, eventually becoming the hereditary domain of the Abe clan (later known as the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]).
Although Yoshimasa lacks the legendary anecdotes associated with his father and elder brother, his appointment as Onmyō no Kami demonstrates his significant skill and accomplishments. Notably, entries in ''{{ill|Shōyūki|ja|小右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyūki|1=Shōyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459316}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Sanesuke|Fujiwara no Sanesuke]] indicate that Yoshimasa continued serving as Tenmon Hakase until his death on [[:en:Kannin|Kan'nin]] 3, April 28 (June 4, 1019).<ref>''Shōyūki''</ref> After his passing, a replacement candidate from Iyo Province, named "Kyuho" (秦久邦), was considered but deemed unqualified due to insufficient knowledge, highlighting a talent shortage and the growing influence of local Onmyōji outside the capital.
== Debate on Heirship == While tradition generally identifies {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}} as Abe no Seimei's eldest son and heir, some scholars propose Yoshimasa as the true heir. This view stems from the nearly identical birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa, as well as Yoshimasa’s appointment as Onmyō no Kami—a role typically reserved for the eldest son in a hereditary system.<ref>Examples of this debate are reflected in modern interpretations, such as the manga ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' by [[:en:Reiko Okano|Reiko Okano]], where Yoshimasa is depicted as Seimei's eldest son.</ref>
However, historical records such as ''Shōyūki'' and ''{{ill|Mido Kanpakuki|ja|御堂関白記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mido Kanpakuki|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488903}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] reference Yoshihira more frequently than Yoshimasa, suggesting that Yoshihira was widely recognized as Seimei’s successor. Additionally, Yoshimasa’s son, {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is sometimes identified as Yoshihira’s biological child adopted by Yoshimasa.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu'' (''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}'').</ref>
== Family == * Father: [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>

He was granted the Rank of ''Jushiijo'' (従四位上). Alongside his elder brother, {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}}, and other prominent Onmyōji such as {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, he played a central role in leading the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the field of Onmyōdō.
== Career == Yoshimasa began his career in [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1 (970), when [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]] recommended him as a ''Tenmon Tokugōsei'' (student specializing in astronomy).<ref>''{{ill|Ruiju Fusenshō|ja|類聚符宣抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruiju Fusenshō|1=Ruijufusenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665188}}''</ref> In [[:en:Kanna (era)|Kanwa]] 2 (986), he succeeded his father as Tenmon Hakase. By [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] 1 (1004), he was appointed Onmyō no Kami, the head of the Onmyōryō.
Historically, the position of Onmyō no Kami had been reserved for prestigious families such as the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}. However, following Yoshimasa’s appointment, the role began alternating between the Abe and Kamo clans, eventually becoming the hereditary domain of the Abe clan (later known as the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]).
Although Yoshimasa lacks the legendary anecdotes associated with his father and elder brother, his appointment as Onmyō no Kami demonstrates his significant skill and accomplishments. Notably, entries in ''{{ill|Shōyūki|ja|小右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyūki|1=Shōyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459316}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Sanesuke|Fujiwara no Sanesuke]] indicate that Yoshimasa continued serving as Tenmon Hakase until his death on [[:en:Kannin|Kan'nin]] 3, April 28 (June 4, 1019).<ref>''Shōyūki''</ref> After his passing, a replacement candidate from Iyo Province, named "Kyuho" (秦久邦), was considered but deemed unqualified due to insufficient knowledge, highlighting a talent shortage and the growing influence of local Onmyōji outside the capital.
== Debate on Heirship == While tradition generally identifies {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}} as Abe no Seimei's eldest son and heir, some scholars propose Yoshimasa as the true heir. This view stems from the nearly identical birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa, as well as Yoshimasa’s appointment as Onmyō no Kami—a role typically reserved for the eldest son in a hereditary system.<ref>Examples of this debate are reflected in modern interpretations, such as the manga ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' by [[:en:Reiko Okano|Reiko Okano]], where Yoshimasa is depicted as Seimei's eldest son.</ref>
However, historical records such as ''Shōyūki'' and ''{{ill|Mido Kanpakuki|ja|御堂関白記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mido Kanpakuki|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488903}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] reference Yoshihira more frequently than Yoshimasa, suggesting that Yoshihira was widely recognized as Seimei’s successor. Additionally, Yoshimasa’s son, {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is sometimes identified as Yoshihira’s biological child adopted by Yoshimasa.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu'' (''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}'').</ref>
== Family == * Father: [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>

However, following Yoshimasa’s appointment, the role began alternating between the Abe and Kamo clans, eventually becoming the hereditary domain of the Abe clan (later known as the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} during the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]).
Although Yoshimasa lacks the legendary anecdotes associated with his father and elder brother, his appointment as Onmyō no Kami demonstrates his significant skill and accomplishments. Notably, entries in ''{{ill|Shōyūki|ja|小右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyūki|1=Shōyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459316}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Sanesuke|Fujiwara no Sanesuke]] indicate that Yoshimasa continued serving as Tenmon Hakase until his death on [[:en:Kannin|Kan'nin]] 3, April 28 (June 4, 1019).<ref>''Shōyūki''</ref> After his passing, a replacement candidate from Iyo Province, named "Kyuho" (秦久邦), was considered but deemed unqualified due to insufficient knowledge, highlighting a talent shortage and the growing influence of local Onmyōji outside the capital.
== Debate on Heirship == While tradition generally identifies {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}} as Abe no Seimei's eldest son and heir, some scholars propose Yoshimasa as the true heir. This view stems from the nearly identical birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa, as well as Yoshimasa’s appointment as Onmyō no Kami—a role typically reserved for the eldest son in a hereditary system.<ref>Examples of this debate are reflected in modern interpretations, such as the manga ''[[:en:Onmyōji|Onmyōji (manga)]]'' by [[:en:Reiko Okano|Reiko Okano]], where Yoshimasa is depicted as Seimei's eldest son.</ref>
However, historical records such as ''Shōyūki'' and ''{{ill|Mido Kanpakuki|ja|御堂関白記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mido Kanpakuki|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488903}}'' by [[:en:Fujiwara no Michinaga|Fujiwara no Michinaga]] reference Yoshihira more frequently than Yoshimasa, suggesting that Yoshihira was widely recognized as Seimei’s successor. Additionally, Yoshimasa’s son, {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is sometimes identified as Yoshihira’s biological child adopted by Yoshimasa.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu'' (''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}'').</ref>
== Family == * Father: [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0955|death_date={{death date|1019|6|4}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11450303]] Wikidata name: Yoshimasa Abe [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Abe no Yoshimasa''' (安倍 吉昌) was a mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} aristocrat, {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji}}, and practitioner of {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}}. He was the second son of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}} (according to some accounts) and held positions such as {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (Doctor of Astronomy), {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Shukeitō|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukeitō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}, and {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. He was granted the Rank of ''Jushiijo'' (従四位上). Alongside his elder brother, {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}}, and other prominent Onmyōji such as {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, he played a central role in leading the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the field of Onmyōdō.
== Career == Yoshimasa began his career in {{ill|Tenroku|en|Tenroku|WD=Q1327229|lt=Tenroku}} 1 (970), when {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori}} recommended him as a ''Tenmon Tokugōsei'' (student specializing in astronomy).<ref>''{{ill|Ruiju Fusenshō|ja|類聚符宣抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruiju Fusenshō|1=Ruijufusenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665188}}''</ref> In {{ill|Kanna (era)|en|Kanna (era)|WD=Q1327053|lt=Kanwa}} 2 (986), he succeeded his father as Tenmon Hakase. By {{ill|Kankō|en|Kankō|WD=Q2736722|lt=Kankō}} 1 (1004), he was appointed Onmyō no Kami, the head of the Onmyōryō.
Historically, the position of Onmyō no Kami had been reserved for prestigious families such as the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}. However, following Yoshimasa’s appointment, the role began alternating between the Abe and Kamo clans, eventually becoming the hereditary domain of the Abe clan (later known as the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}).
Although Yoshimasa lacks the legendary anecdotes associated with his father and elder brother, his appointment as Onmyō no Kami demonstrates his significant skill and accomplishments. Notably, entries in ''{{ill|Shōyūki|ja|小右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyūki|1=Shōyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459316}}'' by {{ill|Fujiwara no Sanesuke|en|Fujiwara no Sanesuke|WD=Q3090659|lt=Fujiwara no Sanesuke}} indicate that Yoshimasa continued serving as Tenmon Hakase until his death on {{ill|Kannin|en|Kannin|WD=Q1193951|lt=Kan'nin}} 3, April 28 (June 4, 1019).<ref>''Shōyūki''</ref> After his passing, a replacement candidate from Iyo Province, named "Kyuho" (秦久邦), was considered but deemed unqualified due to insufficient knowledge, highlighting a talent shortage and the growing influence of local Onmyōji outside the capital.
== Debate on Heirship == While tradition generally identifies {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}} as Abe no Seimei's eldest son and heir, some scholars propose Yoshimasa as the true heir. This view stems from the nearly identical birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa, as well as Yoshimasa’s appointment as Onmyō no Kami—a role typically reserved for the eldest son in a hereditary system.<ref>Examples of this debate are reflected in modern interpretations, such as the manga ''{{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji (manga)}}'' by {{ill|Reiko Okano|en|Reiko Okano|WD=Q463053|lt=Reiko Okano}}, where Yoshimasa is depicted as Seimei's eldest son.</ref>
However, historical records such as ''Shōyūki'' and ''{{ill|Mido Kanpakuki|ja|御堂関白記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mido Kanpakuki|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488903}}'' by {{ill|Fujiwara no Michinaga|en|Fujiwara no Michinaga|WD=Q433133|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga}} reference Yoshihira more frequently than Yoshimasa, suggesting that Yoshihira was widely recognized as Seimei’s successor. Additionally, Yoshimasa’s son, {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is sometimes identified as Yoshihira’s biological child adopted by Yoshimasa.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu'' (''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}'').</ref>
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>

However, following Yoshimasa’s appointment, the role began alternating between the Abe and Kamo clans, eventually becoming the hereditary domain of the Abe clan (later known as the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}).
Although Yoshimasa lacks the legendary anecdotes associated with his father and elder brother, his appointment as Onmyō no Kami demonstrates his significant skill and accomplishments. Notably, entries in ''{{ill|Shōyūki|ja|小右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyūki|1=Shōyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459316}}'' by {{ill|Fujiwara no Sanesuke|en|Fujiwara no Sanesuke|WD=Q3090659|lt=Fujiwara no Sanesuke}} indicate that Yoshimasa continued serving as Tenmon Hakase until his death on {{ill|Kannin|en|Kannin|WD=Q1193951|lt=Kan'nin}} 3, April 28 (June 4, 1019).<ref>''Shōyūki''</ref> After his passing, a replacement candidate from Iyo Province, named "Kyuho" (秦久邦), was considered but deemed unqualified due to insufficient knowledge, highlighting a talent shortage and the growing influence of local Onmyōji outside the capital.
== Debate on Heirship == While tradition generally identifies {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}} as Abe no Seimei's eldest son and heir, some scholars propose Yoshimasa as the true heir. This view stems from the nearly identical birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa, as well as Yoshimasa’s appointment as Onmyō no Kami—a role typically reserved for the eldest son in a hereditary system.<ref>Examples of this debate are reflected in modern interpretations, such as the manga ''{{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji (manga)}}'' by {{ill|Reiko Okano|en|Reiko Okano|WD=Q463053|lt=Reiko Okano}}, where Yoshimasa is depicted as Seimei's eldest son.</ref>
However, historical records such as ''Shōyūki'' and ''{{ill|Mido Kanpakuki|ja|御堂関白記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mido Kanpakuki|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488903}}'' by {{ill|Fujiwara no Michinaga|en|Fujiwara no Michinaga|WD=Q433133|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga}} reference Yoshihira more frequently than Yoshimasa, suggesting that Yoshihira was widely recognized as Seimei’s successor. Additionally, Yoshimasa’s son, {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is sometimes identified as Yoshihira’s biological child adopted by Yoshimasa.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu'' (''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}'').</ref>
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0955|death_date={{death date|1019|6|4}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}} '''Abe no Yoshimasa''' (安倍 吉昌) was a mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} aristocrat, {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji}}, and practitioner of {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō}}. He was the second son of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}} (according to some accounts) and held positions such as {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (Doctor of Astronomy), {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Shukeitō|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukeitō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}, and {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. He was granted the Rank of ''Jushiijo'' (従四位上). Alongside his elder brother, {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}}, and other prominent Onmyōji such as {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, he played a central role in leading the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the field of Onmyōdō.
== Career == Yoshimasa began his career in {{ill|Tenroku|en|Tenroku|WD=Q1327229|lt=Tenroku}} 1 (970), when {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori}} recommended him as a ''Tenmon Tokugōsei'' (student specializing in astronomy).<ref>''{{ill|Ruiju Fusenshō|ja|類聚符宣抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruiju Fusenshō|1=Ruijufusenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665188}}''</ref> In {{ill|Kanna (era)|en|Kanna (era)|WD=Q1327053|lt=Kanwa}} 2 (986), he succeeded his father as Tenmon Hakase. By {{ill|Kankō|en|Kankō|WD=Q2736722|lt=Kankō}} 1 (1004), he was appointed Onmyō no Kami, the head of the Onmyōryō.
Historically, the position of Onmyō no Kami had been reserved for prestigious families such as the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}. However, following Yoshimasa’s appointment, the role began alternating between the Abe and Kamo clans, eventually becoming the hereditary domain of the Abe clan (later known as the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}).
Although Yoshimasa lacks the legendary anecdotes associated with his father and elder brother, his appointment as Onmyō no Kami demonstrates his significant skill and accomplishments. Notably, entries in ''{{ill|Shōyūki|ja|小右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyūki|1=Shōyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459316}}'' by {{ill|Fujiwara no Sanesuke|en|Fujiwara no Sanesuke|WD=Q3090659|lt=Fujiwara no Sanesuke}} indicate that Yoshimasa continued serving as Tenmon Hakase until his death on {{ill|Kannin|en|Kannin|WD=Q1193951|lt=Kan'nin}} 3, April 28 (June 4, 1019).<ref>''Shōyūki''</ref> After his passing, a replacement candidate from Iyo Province, named "Kyuho" (秦久邦), was considered but deemed unqualified due to insufficient knowledge, highlighting a talent shortage and the growing influence of local Onmyōji outside the capital.
== Debate on Heirship == While tradition generally identifies {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}} as Abe no Seimei's eldest son and heir, some scholars propose Yoshimasa as the true heir. This view stems from the nearly identical birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa, as well as Yoshimasa’s appointment as Onmyō no Kami—a role typically reserved for the eldest son in a hereditary system.<ref>Examples of this debate are reflected in modern interpretations, such as the manga ''{{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji (manga)}}'' by {{ill|Reiko Okano|en|Reiko Okano|WD=Q463053|lt=Reiko Okano}}, where Yoshimasa is depicted as Seimei's eldest son.</ref>
However, historical records such as ''Shōyūki'' and ''{{ill|Mido Kanpakuki|ja|御堂関白記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mido Kanpakuki|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488903}}'' by {{ill|Fujiwara no Michinaga|en|Fujiwara no Michinaga|WD=Q433133|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga}} reference Yoshihira more frequently than Yoshimasa, suggesting that Yoshihira was widely recognized as Seimei’s successor. Additionally, Yoshimasa’s son, {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is sometimes identified as Yoshihira’s biological child adopted by Yoshimasa.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu'' (''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}'').</ref>
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>

However, following Yoshimasa’s appointment, the role began alternating between the Abe and Kamo clans, eventually becoming the hereditary domain of the Abe clan (later known as the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}).
Although Yoshimasa lacks the legendary anecdotes associated with his father and elder brother, his appointment as Onmyō no Kami demonstrates his significant skill and accomplishments. Notably, entries in ''{{ill|Shōyūki|ja|小右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyūki|1=Shōyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459316}}'' by {{ill|Fujiwara no Sanesuke|en|Fujiwara no Sanesuke|WD=Q3090659|lt=Fujiwara no Sanesuke|1=Fujiwara no Sanesuke}} indicate that Yoshimasa continued serving as Tenmon Hakase until his death on {{ill|Kannin|en|Kannin|WD=Q1193951|lt=Kan'nin|1=Kannin}} 3, April 28 (June 4, 1019).<ref>''Shōyūki''</ref> After his passing, a replacement candidate from Iyo Province, named "Kyuho" (秦久邦), was considered but deemed unqualified due to insufficient knowledge, highlighting a talent shortage and the growing influence of local Onmyōji outside the capital.
== Debate on Heirship == While tradition generally identifies {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}} as Abe no Seimei's eldest son and heir, some scholars propose Yoshimasa as the true heir. This view stems from the nearly identical birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa, as well as Yoshimasa’s appointment as Onmyō no Kami—a role typically reserved for the eldest son in a hereditary system.<ref>Examples of this debate are reflected in modern interpretations, such as the manga ''{{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji (manga)|1=Onmyōji}}'' by {{ill|Reiko Okano|en|Reiko Okano|WD=Q463053|lt=Reiko Okano|1=Reiko Okano}}, where Yoshimasa is depicted as Seimei's eldest son.</ref>
However, historical records such as ''Shōyūki'' and ''{{ill|Mido Kanpakuki|ja|御堂関白記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mido Kanpakuki|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488903}}'' by {{ill|Fujiwara no Michinaga|en|Fujiwara no Michinaga|WD=Q433133|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|1=Fujiwara no Michinaga}} reference Yoshihira more frequently than Yoshimasa, suggesting that Yoshihira was widely recognized as Seimei’s successor. Additionally, Yoshimasa’s son, {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is sometimes identified as Yoshihira’s biological child adopted by Yoshimasa.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu'' (''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}'').</ref>
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安倍吉昌]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0955|death_date={{death date|1019|6|4}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}} '''Abe no Yoshimasa''' (安倍 吉昌) was a mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} aristocrat, {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji|1=Onmyōji}}, and practitioner of {{ill|Onmyōdō|en|Onmyōdō|WD=Q1340540|lt=Onmyōdō|1=Onmyōdō}}. He was the second son of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}} (according to some accounts) and held positions such as {{ill|Tenmon Hakase|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} (Doctor of Astronomy), {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Shukeitō|ja|主計頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukeitō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主計寮|1=shukeiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368792}}, and {{ill|Onmyō no Kami|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō no Kami|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}. He was granted the Rank of ''Jushiijo'' (従四位上). Alongside his elder brother, {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}}, and other prominent Onmyōji such as {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}}, he played a central role in leading the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} and the field of Onmyōdō.
== Career == Yoshimasa began his career in {{ill|Tenroku|en|Tenroku|WD=Q1327229|lt=Tenroku|1=Tenroku}} 1 (970), when {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|en|Kamo no Yasunori|WD=Q6358935|lt=Kamo no Yasunori|1=Kamo no Yasunori}} recommended him as a ''Tenmon Tokugōsei'' (student specializing in astronomy).<ref>''{{ill|Ruiju Fusenshō|ja|類聚符宣抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruiju Fusenshō|1=Ruijufusenshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665188}}''</ref> In {{ill|Kanna (era)|en|Kanna (era)|WD=Q1327053|lt=Kanwa|1=Kanna (era)}} 2 (986), he succeeded his father as Tenmon Hakase. By {{ill|Kankō|en|Kankō|WD=Q2736722|lt=Kankō|1=Kankō}} 1 (1004), he was appointed Onmyō no Kami, the head of the Onmyōryō.
Historically, the position of Onmyō no Kami had been reserved for prestigious families such as the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}. However, following Yoshimasa’s appointment, the role began alternating between the Abe and Kamo clans, eventually becoming the hereditary domain of the Abe clan (later known as the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}).
Although Yoshimasa lacks the legendary anecdotes associated with his father and elder brother, his appointment as Onmyō no Kami demonstrates his significant skill and accomplishments. Notably, entries in ''{{ill|Shōyūki|ja|小右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyūki|1=Shōyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459316}}'' by {{ill|Fujiwara no Sanesuke|en|Fujiwara no Sanesuke|WD=Q3090659|lt=Fujiwara no Sanesuke|1=Fujiwara no Sanesuke}} indicate that Yoshimasa continued serving as Tenmon Hakase until his death on {{ill|Kannin|en|Kannin|WD=Q1193951|lt=Kan'nin|1=Kannin}} 3, April 28 (June 4, 1019).<ref>''Shōyūki''</ref> After his passing, a replacement candidate from Iyo Province, named "Kyuho" (秦久邦), was considered but deemed unqualified due to insufficient knowledge, highlighting a talent shortage and the growing influence of local Onmyōji outside the capital.
== Debate on Heirship == While tradition generally identifies {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}} as Abe no Seimei's eldest son and heir, some scholars propose Yoshimasa as the true heir. This view stems from the nearly identical birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa, as well as Yoshimasa’s appointment as Onmyō no Kami—a role typically reserved for the eldest son in a hereditary system.<ref>Examples of this debate are reflected in modern interpretations, such as the manga ''{{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji (manga)|1=Onmyōji}}'' by {{ill|Reiko Okano|en|Reiko Okano|WD=Q463053|lt=Reiko Okano|1=Reiko Okano}}, where Yoshimasa is depicted as Seimei's eldest son.</ref>
However, historical records such as ''Shōyūki'' and ''{{ill|Mido Kanpakuki|ja|御堂関白記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mido Kanpakuki|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488903}}'' by {{ill|Fujiwara no Michinaga|en|Fujiwara no Michinaga|WD=Q433133|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|1=Fujiwara no Michinaga}} reference Yoshihira more frequently than Yoshimasa, suggesting that Yoshihira was widely recognized as Seimei’s successor. Additionally, Yoshimasa’s son, {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is sometimes identified as Yoshihira’s biological child adopted by Yoshimasa.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu'' (''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}'').</ref>
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>

By {{ill|Kankō|en|Kankō|WD=Q2736722|lt=Kankō|1=Kankō}} 1 (1004), he was appointed Onmyō no Kami, the head of the Onmyōryō.
Historically, the position of Onmyō no Kami had been reserved for prestigious families such as the {{ill|Kamo clan|ja|賀茂氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōnakatomi clan|ja|大中臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnakatomi clan|qq=}}. However, following Yoshimasa’s appointment, the role began alternating between the Abe and Kamo clans, eventually becoming the hereditary domain of the Abe clan (later known as the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}} during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}).
Although Yoshimasa lacks the legendary anecdotes associated with his father and elder brother, his appointment as Onmyō no Kami demonstrates his significant skill and accomplishments. Notably, entries in ''{{ill|Shōyūki|ja|小右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyūki|1=Shōyūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459316}}'' by {{ill|Fujiwara no Sanesuke|en|Fujiwara no Sanesuke|WD=Q3090659|lt=Fujiwara no Sanesuke|1=Fujiwara no Sanesuke}} indicate that Yoshimasa continued serving as Tenmon Hakase until his death on {{ill|Kannin|en|Kannin|WD=Q1193951|lt=Kan'nin|1=Kannin}} 3, April 28 (June 4, 1019).<ref>''Shōyūki''</ref> After his passing, a replacement candidate from Iyo Province, named "Kyuho" (秦久邦), was considered but deemed unqualified due to insufficient knowledge, highlighting a talent shortage and the growing influence of local Onmyōji outside the capital.
== Debate on Heirship == While tradition generally identifies {{ill|Abe no Yoshihira|ja|安倍吉平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Yoshihira|qq=}} as Abe no Seimei's eldest son and heir, some scholars propose Yoshimasa as the true heir. This view stems from the nearly identical birth years of Yoshihira and Yoshimasa, as well as Yoshimasa’s appointment as Onmyō no Kami—a role typically reserved for the eldest son in a hereditary system.<ref>Examples of this debate are reflected in modern interpretations, such as the manga ''{{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=Onmyōji (manga)|1=Onmyōji}}'' by {{ill|Reiko Okano|en|Reiko Okano|WD=Q463053|lt=Reiko Okano|1=Reiko Okano}}, where Yoshimasa is depicted as Seimei's eldest son.</ref>
However, historical records such as ''Shōyūki'' and ''{{ill|Mido Kanpakuki|ja|御堂関白記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mido Kanpakuki|1=Midō Kanpakuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488903}}'' by {{ill|Fujiwara no Michinaga|en|Fujiwara no Michinaga|WD=Q433133|lt=Fujiwara no Michinaga|1=Fujiwara no Michinaga}} reference Yoshihira more frequently than Yoshimasa, suggesting that Yoshihira was widely recognized as Seimei’s successor. Additionally, Yoshimasa’s son, {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is sometimes identified as Yoshihira’s biological child adopted by Yoshimasa.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu'' (''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}'').</ref>
== Family == * Father: {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}} * Mother: Unknown * Wife: Unknown * Children: ** Son: {{ill|Abe no Narichika|ja|安倍成親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Narichika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Some genealogies suggest that Narichika was Yoshihira's biological son adopted by Yoshimasa. Another child, "Hōshin," appears in certain sources but is also believed to have been adopted.<ref>''Abe-shi Keizu,'' in ''{{ill|Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja|続群書類従|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Gunsho Ruijū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 群書類従|1=Gunsho Ruijū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1554852}}.''</ref>
